workflow.hint: Telar sites are built in six steps, from setup to publishing. The tiles below show you the state of each part.

tip.configure: Configure your site's settings here — its name, theme, language, and a short description, along with author details, hosting, and how your stories and collection appear to readers.
tip.objects: Add or modify the images, videos, and audio files that your site uses. You can either upload them or integrate them from IIIF, Vimeo, YouTube and Google Drive.
tip.stories: Stories are the core of Telar sites, pairing a series of questions and short answers with a framed view of an object, plus up to two layer panels for further information.
tip.glossary: You can add additional panels with definitions of key terms, concepts, people, places, or anything else you like. Manage them here and use them in your stories and pages.
tip.pages: Your Telar site has a homepage and can include additional pages, such as an About, Credits, or Bibliography page. Edit them here.
tip.publish_collaborator: Only the convenor can publish the site. When your changes are ready, let them know.
tip.publish_convenor: When you're ready, bundle your edits into a commit to GitHub, where your site rebuilds in about two minutes.

activity.empty: No edits yet. When you or a teammate touches a story, an object, or a term, it will show up here.

recovery.body: {{N}} story files exist in your GitHub repo but aren't in the compositor. They likely came from a hand-edit on GitHub or a teammate working outside the editor. Restore them here as drafts, or ignore.

from_docs.hint: Short excerpts from the guides, to read without leaving the compositor. The full documentation lives at <docsLink>telar.org/docs</docsLink>.
from_docs.desc_start: Sign in, connect a repo, and make your first edits.
from_docs.desc_intro: What the Compositor is and how it works.
from_docs.desc_configure: Set your site's name, theme, language, and description.
from_docs.desc_narrative: How stories, steps, and panels fit together.
from_docs.desc_markdown: Format panel text — emphasis, links, lists.
from_docs.desc_iiif: Use high-resolution images from museums, libraries, and archives.
from_docs.desc_objects: Browse, edit, upload, and add objects.
from_docs.desc_publish: Review changes, commit to GitHub, track the build.
from_docs.desc_stories: Build a story with the visual editor.
from_docs.desc_video: Capture clips and set loop points for media steps.
from_docs.desc_refine: Set image coordinates and polish your exhibition.
from_docs.desc_sync: Re-sync with your repository and upgrade Telar.
from_docs.desc_glossary: Define terms and link to them with [[term]].
from_docs.desc_pages: Build your homepage and standalone pages like About, Credits, or Bibliography.

checklist.convenor_heading: Set up your project — three things first
checklist.convenor_step1: Confirm site title and theme in Site settings — Sets the language, theme, and what visitors see on the published landing page.
checklist.convenor_step2: Add your first object — Upload an image, paste an IIIF manifest, or link external media. Objects are what stories point at.
checklist.convenor_step3: Invite your team and write a first story — A story is a sequence of steps that zoom and pan around objects with your analysis.
checklist.collaborator_heading: Today, three things to get started
checklist.collaborator_step1: Start with “What is the compositor?” — See how Telar pages, objects, stories, and the glossary fit together.
checklist.collaborator_step2: Browse the Objects tab — See what the convenor has set up so far. Pick one to read closely.
checklist.collaborator_step3: Open a story step and start writing — Your edits save automatically. Nothing goes live until the convenor publishes.
